agent_notes: Quality-constrained longlist: Hounslow is strongest for markets, historic houses, industrial/music museums and two PAINTS-approved park walks; true Books & Coffee venues are scarce under the v6 definition.
Creative Walks only use Osterley Park & House and Gunnersbury Park from the PAINTS v6 Premium Launch Parks list.

Google ratings/review counts, live Google hours and exact group walk-in tolerance could not be reliably extracted in this environment; host must re-check Google Maps, social posts and availability before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Park generally daily 07:00-dusk; house, garden, shop and cafe run seasonal National Trust hours. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm exact 2026 event-date hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; paid house/garden entry and cafe group seating are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 in daylight on formal visitor routes; avoid dusk, isolated paths and event days with private hire.
venues.capacity_notes: Route stops: Robert Adam house facade, stable yard, garden/lake, meadow and long-view approach.
venues.why_fits_paints: Osterley is the borough's cleanest PAINTS walk anchor: grand but not overused, story-rich, and already on the approved safety list. The visual rhythm moves from Tube suburb to neoclassical estate to quiet designed landscape.
venues.anecdote_story: The Child banking family estate was remodelled by Robert Adam; the National Trust now presents it as one of London's last surviving country estates.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, Adam architecture, lake views, stable yard and optional house interiors.
venues.safety_notes: Osterley Park & House is on the PAINTS Premium Launch Parks list. Keep to daylight visitor routes and confirm any closures or events.
venues.backup_venue: Osterley Park Stables Cafe / National Trust visitor centre
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of the stable-yard start point
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather backup is cafe/visitor-centre rather than a same-category walk; group seating and walk-in tolerance are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.venue_name: Gunnersbury Park House, Temple and Lake Walk
venues.neighbourhood_station: Gunnersbury Park / Acton Town / Brentford fringe
venues.opening_hours: Park daily 07:00-dusk; museum Tue-Sun 10:00-16:30. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; free museum/cafe group walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a compact daylight walk using high-footfall paths around the museum, temple, lake and lawns.
venues.capacity_notes: Route stops: Rothschild mansion, museum threshold, Italian garden/temple, round pond/lake and orangery area.
venues.why_fits_paints: Gunnersbury gives Hounslow a safe, visually varied walk with a museum backup already on site. Its centenary of public ownership gives the host a current civic story rather than just a pretty-park stroll.
venues.anecdote_story: The estate moved from private country-house grounds to public park in 1926, and the museum interprets the shared histories of Ealing and Hounslow.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, historic house exterior, lake and optional local-history collection.
venues.safety_notes: Gunnersbury Park is on the PAINTS Premium Launch Parks list. Daytime only; do not extend into unapproved waterside or isolated routes.
venues.backup_venue: Gunnersbury Park Museum cafe / Pure in the Park cafe
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of the museum-side start point
venues.risks_watchouts: Park straddles Hounslow/Ealing administration; keep the Hounslow/Brentford framing clear and confirm cafe capacity.

venues.opening_hours: First Sunday of each month, usually 09:00-15:30. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm next market date and trader list.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting-point cafe walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for a drifting 8-12 group if hosted early before peak crowding.
venues.capacity_notes: Use Old Market Place as the spine; split into pairs for stalls and regroup at Turnham Green Terrace.
venues.why_fits_paints: A community-led flower market has instant visual payoff and an easy show-and-tell mechanic around growers, seasonal stems and domestic rituals. It feels local rather than tourist-scripted.
venues.anecdote_story: The market was launched by residents to revive Old Market Place as a civic high-street destination.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Flowers, plants, floristry, growers and nearby independent cafes/shops.
venues.safety_notes: Busy high-street setting with strong daytime footfall and Tube/bus exits; watch crowding and road crossings.
venues.backup_walk_time: Same Old Market Place location; monthly schedule differs, so use as calendar backup rather than same-day backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and monthly scheduling are the main risks; no same-category backup operates every Sunday in the same slot.

venues.opening_hours: Monthly Sunday market, usually 09:00-15:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm 2026 date and trader list.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting-point cafe walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 as a browsing-and-story format; keep a clear regroup point.
venues.capacity_notes: Street-market flow supports pairs and small clusters better than one large moving block.
venues.why_fits_paints: Vintage objects create easy prompts for strangers: provenance, taste, memory and value. The market has a more characterful texture than generic food stalls.
venues.anecdote_story: Part of the resident-led Chiswick market revival around Old Market Place.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Antiques, vintage homewares, prints, fashion, collectibles and nearby cafes.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime high-street location; manage traffic crossings and crowding.
venues.backup_walk_time: Same Old Market Place location; monthly schedule differs, so use as calendar backup rather than same-day backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather exposure and monthly schedule require date-specific confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Monthly Sunday market, usually 09:30-15:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm 2026 date and producers.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting-point cafe walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if run as a tasting-and-producer conversation, not a static lunch.
venues.capacity_notes: Best as short producer stops plus a seated coffee/wine debrief nearby.
venues.why_fits_paints: The specificity is the point: a whole market around cheese gives the host a playful but substantive theme. It suits strangers because each stall naturally invites opinion and story.
venues.anecdote_story: The market grew from Chiswick's resident-led market programme and focuses on specialist cheesemakers and affineurs.
venues.serve_or_on_view: British and European cheeses, accompaniments, producers and occasional hot food.
venues.safety_notes: Busy daytime high-street setting; crowding is more likely than personal-safety risk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Same Old Market Place location; schedule differs, so use as calendar backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and dairy-specific dietary limitations; confirm trader attendance.

venues.opening_hours: Monthly street-food market. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm 2026 dates, hours and traders.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting-point cafe walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Usable for 8-12 but less PAINTS-specific than flowers, antiques or cheese.
venues.capacity_notes: Plan for standing/browsing, with seated backup nearby.
venues.why_fits_paints: Good operationally and community-led, though the cultural hook is weaker than Chiswick's object- or producer-led markets. Works best when framed around small makers and food memories.
venues.anecdote_story: One strand of the Chiswick High Road market revival around Old Market Place.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, drinks and local traders.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime, high-footfall street setting.
venues.backup_walk_time: Same Old Market Place location; schedule differs, so use as calendar backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: More food-court-adjacent than PAINTS-perfect; verify it is not just a generic stall mix on the chosen date.

venues.venue_name: The Food Market Chiswick, Dukes Meadows
venues.opening_hours: Sunday market, typically 10:00-14:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date operation.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers/food market; meeting-point coffee walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a calm Sunday group; less crowded than central Chiswick markets.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor market with food stalls; use a clear meeting point at the school/market entrance.
venues.why_fits_paints: Producer-led food has an easy conversational structure, and the riverside/meadow edge gives it a softer west-London feel. It is less obvious than the High Road markets.
venues.anecdote_story: Long-running community food market at Dukes Meadows supporting independent traders and local food culture.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farmers-market produce, baked goods, coffee, hot food and seasonal stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime market with family/community footfall; avoid making the Thames Path an extended unsourced walk.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by local route/transit depending on meeting point; host must verify live directions.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather exposure and exact stall list vary.

venues.opening_hours: Selected Sundays/seasonal market dates. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current 2026 schedule.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market when running; cafe/group meeting-point policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good when confirmed running, but schedule risk keeps it Tier B.
venues.capacity_notes: Use the market as browsing plus optional garden/cafe pause; do not source Chiswick House grounds as a Creative Walk because it is not on the PAINTS safety list.
venues.why_fits_paints: Craft and food stalls against the Palladian-house setting are visually strong. The issue is operational certainty rather than taste.
venues.anecdote_story: Duck Pond Markets are maker-led community markets that rotate through heritage and park settings.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft, food, vintage and maker stalls when scheduled.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime heritage-site setting; use the market only, not an unsourced park walk.
venues.backup_venue: The Food Market Chiswick, Dukes Meadows
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by local route/transit; host must verify live directions.
venues.risks_watchouts: Schedule variability and the non-approved park-walk boundary.

venues.opening_hours: Sunday community market dates/hours vary. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm 2026 schedule.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market when running; meeting-point cafe walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Acceptable for a local-community market format but lower aesthetic ceiling than Chiswick markets.
venues.capacity_notes: Use as a Brentford-local option, ideally paired with a short cultural anchor nearby.
venues.why_fits_paints: It gives geographic balance and a civic Brentford story. It is more useful as a warm local backup than a flagship PAINTS pick.
venues.anecdote_story: Brentford's market tradition is tied to its old high-street and waterside trading identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local food, produce, crafts and community stalls depending on date.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime town-centre setting; check current high-street works and crowding.
venues.backup_venue: Watermans Brentford market/craft-event programming or London Museum of Water & Steam event days
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 15 min by local walk/transit depending on exact market/event; host must verify.
venues.risks_watchouts: Schedule and trader quality are variable; do not use unless the date has a confirmed curated market.

venues.opening_hours: Tue-Sun 12:00-17:00, Mon closed. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date hours and any exhibition changeover.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum; group 8-12 walk-in tolerance and any guided-tour limits are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a compact, hosted visit; small rooms mean the group may need to move in clusters.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic house scale, best for 8-10 rather than a large static gathering.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the borough's best art-history pick: intimate, domestic and full of narrative hooks. It lets attendees meet Hogarth as a working London image-maker, not just a textbook name.
venues.anecdote_story: William Hogarth used the Chiswick house as his country home; the house now displays prints and stories around his life, satire and London visual culture.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Hogarth prints, period rooms, garden and temporary exhibition programme.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Chiswick location near major roads and transport; avoid late finishes because the site is small and closes early.
venues.backup_venue: Chiswick House and Gardens Trust exhibitions/cafe
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5-8 min; host must verify live directions and whether a qualifying exhibition is open.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small-room capacity and current exhibition strength need host confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Seasonal National Trust house/garden hours. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm exact 2026 date, paid-entry availability and any closed rooms.
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed/paid National Trust entry likely; group 8-12 walk-in availability is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if entry and room flow are checked in advance; reservation uncertainty caps it at Tier B.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic-house circulation; not ideal for a single static discussion.
venues.best_time_slot: Late morning weekend, avoiding school-holiday peaks.
venues.why_fits_paints: The interiors and Adam architecture are high-character and story-rich. It is less operationally loose than the park walk because house access and room closures must be checked.
venues.anecdote_story: Osterley was redesigned by Robert Adam for the Child banking family and remains one of London's grandest surviving country houses.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic interiors, decorative arts, gardens and any National Trust temporary displays.
venues.safety_notes: Approved park setting; keep to official visitor routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying museum/gallery backup within 10 min; closest qualifying alternative is Boston Manor House by transit/taxi, roughly 20-30 min depending on route.
venues.risks_watchouts: No tight same-category backup; paid-entry/timed access must be verified.

venues.opening_hours: Public opening days/hours vary by season and event programme. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free/heritage-house access when open; group walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for small guided heritage group if open; less robust as a spontaneous walk-in.
venues.capacity_notes: Restored Jacobean house rooms; group should move gently through rooms.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekend afternoon on confirmed open day.
venues.why_fits_paints: A restored Jacobean house in Brentford is exactly the kind of overlooked local texture PAINTS likes. It needs a stronger current exhibition hook to become Tier A.
venues.anecdote_story: The house dates to the 17th century and reopened after a major restoration project led by Hounslow.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic interiors, local heritage interpretation and occasional exhibition/event programming.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; Boston Manor Park is not on the PAINTS Creative Walk safety list, so do not turn this into a park walk.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying museum/gallery backup within 10 min; closest qualifying alternative is roughly 20 min walk or short bus/taxi.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening schedule and exhibition programme are the weak points.

venues.opening_hours: Typically Thu-Sun and school holidays 10:00-16:00, with special steaming/event days. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm exact 2026 event-date hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Paid museum entry; group 8-12 walk-in/ticket availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 if tickets are checked; big machinery gives natural discussion stops.
venues.capacity_notes: Industrial museum spaces are more forgiving than small historic houses.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 10:30-12:30, ideally on a steaming/event day.
venues.why_fits_paints: The huge beam engines and waterworks story bring craft, engineering and city infrastructure into the same room. It is tactile and odd enough to feel like a discovery.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum occupies the former Kew Bridge Waterworks and tells the story of London's water supply through working steam engines.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic pumping engines, industrial architecture, waterworks interpretation and family/event programming.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Kew Bridge/Brentford location; confirm access routes from station.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2-4 min walk
venues.risks_watchouts: Steaming-day schedules matter; some attendees may read it as engineering rather than art/craft unless hosted well.

venues.opening_hours: Museum opening and tour times vary; commonly Thu-Sun with guided tours. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current 2026 hours and tour availability.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum/tour entry; group 8-12 walk-in availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a hosted show-and-tell museum day if tour capacity is available.
venues.capacity_notes: Instrument demonstrations and Wurlitzer-style theatre organ moments can anchor conversation.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday early afternoon aligned to tour times.
venues.why_fits_paints: Mechanical music is a high-story, high-delight subject that strangers can react to immediately. It is niche without being cold.
venues.anecdote_story: The collection traces self-playing instruments, music boxes, player pianos and theatre organs from domestic parlours to cinemas.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Mechanical musical instruments, demonstrations, concerts and guided tours.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Brentford High Street/Kew Bridge setting; strong paired backup nearby.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2-4 min walk
venues.risks_watchouts: Tour schedule and walk-in capacity must be confirmed.

venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if the special exhibition is active; otherwise better as a walk backup than a standalone museum day.
venues.capacity_notes: Local-history rooms plus exhibition spaces in a restored mansion.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Rothschild-house setting and west-London collection have charm, but the current-exhibition hook needs checking. It works best paired with the approved park walk.
venues.anecdote_story: Gunnersbury was once a private estate and now presents the shared histories of Ealing and Hounslow.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local history, design/social-history collection, and rotating exhibitions.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Beautiful independent antiquarian bookshop, but no verified on-site cafe or coffee partner literally next door under PAINTS v6 Books & Coffee rules.
Independent-feeling Chiswick bookshop signal, but no verified cafe/on-site coffee and group dwell capacity found.
Book offer is substantive, but it is a chain charity shop and lacks on-site/next-door coffee evidence for a PAINTS Books & Coffee event.
Strong wine-taste fit, but it appears primarily as a retail wine shop/tasting programme rather than a walk-in drinks venue for 8-12.
Atmospheric riverside pub, but the location sits outside the Hounslow borough line in Hammersmith/Chiswick fringe for this district assignment.
Operationally plausible, but current sources did not show enough curated maker/producer identity or PAINTS-level atmosphere.
Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list; therefore cannot be sourced even though locally important.
Historic and visually strong, but not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list; could be reconsidered only via team safety review, not sourced here.
